mechanism_narrative: Persistent growth in real GDP (GDPC1) signals strong economic demand, which boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in periods of GDPC1 growth are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates monetary easing, reducing borrowing costs and boosting equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production (INDPRO) and declines in CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) signal favorable supply-side dynamics, reducing corporate costs and boosting equity returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in interaction periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Monetary tightening cycles (FEDFUNDS > 4.5%) combined with rising inflation (CPIAUCSL > 2.5%) create headwinds for equities, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in tightening periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC > 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) indicates robust labor market conditions, increasing consumer spending and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in high PAYEMS periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in unemployment (UNRATE) below 3.5% signals labor market overheating, prompting central bank intervention and negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in low UNRATE periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC > 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) reflects strong consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in demand surge periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production (INDPRO) amid rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals supply shocks, reducing corporate margins and causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in supply shock periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC > 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
